outlook.headline: Silicon photonics demand is broadening into a real ramp
outlook.summary: Reads are uniformly bullish and the mention trend is rising to 0.8x, suggesting silicon photonics is moving from early interest to broader deployment over the next 1-2 quarters. The strongest pull is AI/HPC and Tier 1 network adoption, with tight supply and capacity needs showing up at key enablers like GF, Veeco, Marvell, Credo and Teradyne. Beneficiaries are the suppliers tied to hot demand and ramping volumes; watch for whether this converts into sustained production orders rather than just early design wins.

drivers.label: AI/HPC-driven demand inflection
drivers.detail: Strong AI compute and AI/HPC networking demand is driving silicon photonics volumes higher.

drivers.label: Ramp from early adoption to production
drivers.detail: Multiple reads point to starting volumes, ramping production test, and large-account/multi-unit orders.

drivers.label: Tight supply and capacity needs
drivers.detail: Tight supply and capacity expansion signal a constrained but favorable backdrop for suppliers.

drivers.label: Broader optical roadmap penetration
drivers.detail: Silicon photonics is appearing across optical roadmaps and architectures, indicating widening product mix participation.

drivers.label: Rising mention momentum
drivers.detail: Company mentions are trending up, ending at 0.8x with 33% momentum versus trailing average, consistent with strengthening attention and adoption.

risks: Any easing of tight supply could temper the urgency and pricing benefit for suppliers.
Early ramp status means timing risk remains if volume conversion slows after initial orders.
If AI/HPC deployment pauses, the current broad enthusiasm could revert to design-win noise rather than shipment growth.
